title: Capital Core Phase 1 Specification
purpose: Implementation-ready data contracts for Capital Bridge Phase 1 foundation: canonical capital state, model output normalization, execution plan primitives, and reconciliation baseline.

status.rollout_rule: Phase 1 contracts must ship before dashboard becomes system authority.

global_conventions.decimal_policy: Store high precision decimals as strings in persistence; cast to number in app layer where safe.
global_conventions.soft_delete_policy: Use deleted_at nullable timestamp for records requiring auditability.

capital_graph.description: Canonical state container for one client profile.

entities.description: People or legal entities linked to the capital graph.

capital_snapshot.description: Periodic aggregate state from canonical entities.
determinism_rules: For identical input state, snapshot output must be identical.
No hidden randomness, no time-dependent mutation, and no partial-read aggregation.
Snapshot generation must run after a committed state write boundary.
inclusion_rules: Only records with deleted_at IS NULL are included.
Only records with as_of <= capital_graph.as_of are included.
All records must match the same capital_graph_id.
Snapshot must be computed against a consistent read of all tables within a single transaction boundary.
transaction_requirements: Snapshot must execute within a single database transaction.
Transaction isolation level must guarantee a consistent snapshot (REPEATABLE READ or stronger).
All aggregation queries must execute within the same transaction context.
No external reads or cross-request aggregation allowed.
capital_snapshot.transaction_isolation_operational_note: Isolation is primarily enforced via version pinning and transactional RPC execution. Database isolation level should be configured at the infrastructure level to guarantee consistent reads; do not rely on SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL inside the function body/header.
anchor_enforcement_rules: Snapshot must only be generated after capital_graph.version is finalized for the write operation.
Snapshot queries must explicitly filter by capital_graph_id AND capital_graph_version.
No 'latest state' queries are allowed in snapshot generation.
validation_expectations: computed_from.assets_count must equal actual counted rows used in aggregation.
computed_from.liabilities_count must equal actual counted rows used in aggregation.
computed_from.income_streams_count must equal actual counted rows used in aggregation.
computed_from.obligations_count must equal actual counted rows used in aggregation.
If any count mismatch or null aggregation occurs, snapshot generation must fail rather than return partial data.
capital_snapshot.domain_completeness_boundary: Snapshot validity is structural (internal consistency) and does not require income presence. Economic completeness requirements (for example income-required flows) are enforced at model/Lion precondition layers.

model_run_snapshot.description: Normalized output envelope for each model run.

model_preconditions_contract.purpose: Model-layer domain completeness rules. These are enforced at model execution time, not snapshot generation time.
model_preconditions_contract.run_state_authority: model_runs.status is authoritative for run state. output_normalized carries explanatory payload and metrics, but does not override model_runs.status.

invalid_run_state.reason_shape: string (single taxonomy code) OR string[] (multiple codes, deduped and sorted in API responses)

run_state_invariants: If model_runs.status = invalid_preconditions, output_normalized.metrics MUST be null.
If model_runs.status = invalid_preconditions, output_normalized.model_specific_payload MUST be null only (empty object is not allowed; avoids dual semantics downstream).
If model_runs.status = completed, output_normalized.metrics MUST NOT be null.
If model_runs.status = completed, all model-required metric keys for that model_key MUST be present and non-null (no partial completion).

lion_invalid_preconditions_contract.behavior: Lion maps output_normalized.reason (taxonomy string[]) deterministically to three blocks: what_is_happening, what_will_happen, what_must_be_done. Full lion_status mapping, signal→band→status rules, tier slicing, and headline determinism are defined only in authority_doc. No invented numbers, inferred income, or inferred expenses.
non_goals: Do not reinterpret taxonomy codes into new semantics.
Do not soften refusal into speculative yield or expense estimates.

execution_action_schema.description: Manual-first execution coordination primitives (no custody, no direct trade execution in platform).

reconciliation_schema.description: Baseline expected-versus-actual comparison contract powering alerts.

minimal_api_contracts_phase1.description: Implementation bootstrap endpoints; map to Next.js route handlers or service layer.

phase1_execution_constraints.intent: Prevent Step 1 overbuild and preserve delivery velocity.
brutal_constraints: Step 1 (core_state_bootstrap) must be completable in <= 2-3 engineering days.
If Step 1 exceeds this window, reduce scope immediately rather than expanding architecture.
Phase 1 assumes one capital_graph per user for operational proof.
Do not optimize multi-entity ownership graphs in Step 1.
step1_strict_scope_excludes: capital_entities table usage beyond defaulting entity_id to user context
snapshot and model-run tables beyond what Step 2/3 require
phase1_execution_constraints.temporary_simplification_rule: For Step 1, entity_id may map to the user context directly; full multi-entity semantics are deferred.

a_live_progress: [A-LIVE] Step 1 migration is applied: advisory_v2.capital_graphs, capital_assets, capital_liabilities, capital_income_streams, capital_obligations, and upsert_capital_graph_snapshot are live in Supabase.

c_decisions_remaining.question: Should cross-model metrics (for example coverage) become canonical or remain model-scoped permanently?
c_decisions_remaining.interim_rule: Continue with metric_semantic_scope and explicit model-scoped labels.

c_decisions_remaining.question: How should conflicts be resolved when partner_feed and user-confirmed values disagree?
c_decisions_remaining.interim_rule: Use source_of_truth_priority ordering, log conflict, require explicit operator resolution.

c_decisions_remaining.question: Who can override partner values and under what approvals/audit requirements?
c_decisions_remaining.interim_rule: No silent overrides; all overrides require audit event with actor and reason.

a_live_structural_gaps: [A-LIVE] Decision engine not implemented: no automated transformation from model outputs to execution actions.
[A-LIVE] Reconciliation-to-execution linkage not implemented: recommended actions are not rule-generated yet.
[A-LIVE] Timeline authority incomplete: no canonical rollback/version stitching API finalized.

phase1_delivery_playbook.objective: Ship a minimal, truthful, operational backbone before dashboard authority work.
anti_stall_rules: Do not expand schema surface before first working vertical slice.
Do not add new enum families unless directly required by active implementation.
Do not optimize naming or abstraction beyond immediate build needs.

strict_build_sequence.name: core_state_bootstrap
scope: Create tables: capital_graphs, capital_assets, capital_liabilities, capital_income_streams, capital_obligations.
Apply single-user assumption and defer entities/partner_links complexity.
exit_criteria: One user capital graph can be created and updated end-to-end.
Assets, liabilities, income streams, and obligations persist with versioned graph context.
Implementation completes within 2-3 engineering days; otherwise scope is reduced.

strict_build_sequence.name: snapshot_baseline
non_goals: No dashboard or reporting UX work.
No analytics expansion beyond required aggregate snapshot fields.
No partner integration or execution layer work in this step.
scope: Create capital_snapshots table and basic aggregate computation.
Persist snapshot totals (assets, liabilities, net worth, monthly income, obligations, surplus).
Bind each snapshot to capital_graph_id + version.
exit_criteria: At least one deterministic snapshot can be generated from stored core records.
Re-running snapshot generation for unchanged capital_graph_id + version produces identical values.
Snapshot generation fails explicitly on any validation_expectations breach (no partial or fallback output).
No downstream service reads aggregate data from base tables once snapshot is introduced.

strict_build_sequence.name: single_model_wiring
scope: Write normalized output to capital_model_run_snapshots using model_run_snapshot contract.
exit_criteria: Model run can be recorded with output_normalized and metric_semantic_scope.

strict_build_sequence.name: manual_execution_actions
scope: Support create, status transition, and audit trail events.
exit_criteria: Execution action can move through allowed state transitions with immutable event history.

strict_build_sequence.name: minimal_reconciliation
scope: Implement reconciliation generation for one period.
Compute at least income_gap variance and emit at least one alert contract record.
exit_criteria: System can produce one reconciliation and one alert from expected vs actual snapshots.

phase1_delivery_playbook.stop_condition_before_phase2: After Step 5 succeeds in production-like environment, pause and review before any unified dashboard authority rollout.

implementation_acceptance_criteria: At least one canonical capital_graph per user can be created and versioned.
Each model can write normalized outputs into model_run_snapshot contract.
Execution actions can be created and progressed through valid status transitions with audit events.
A reconciliation can be generated for a period using expected and actual snapshots.
At least one alert can be generated from reconciliation variance.
Dashboard work may begin only after these criteria are met.
Phase 2 authority rollout is blocked until phase1_delivery_playbook Step 1-5 exit criteria are satisfied.
